name: Reuben Quiche in Rye Bread Crust
recipeInstructions:
Conventional method:
1. Break up the toasted rye bread into crumbs.
2. In a saucepan, melt the margarine over low heat until melted.
3. Add the breadcrumbs to the melted butter and mix well.
4. Press the mixture evenly into a pie plate.
5. Sprinkle shredded cheese over the crust.
6. Add the shredded corn beef and drained sauerkraut on top of the cheese.
7. In a bowl, whisk together the half and half (or milk), eggs, grated onion, and Thousand Island dressing until well combined.
8. Pour the mixture over the filling in the pie plate.
9. Bake at 375°F for 35-40 minutes or until a knife inserted into the center comes out clean.
Microwave method:
1. Make bread crumbs by breaking up the toasted rye bread.
2. In the microwave, melt the margarine in the pie plate for 45-60 seconds until melted.
3. Add the breadcrumbs to the melted butter and spread evenly onto the bottom and sides of the pie plate.
4. Sprinkle shredded cheese over the crust.
5. Add the shredded corn beef and sauerkraut on top of the cheese.
6. Whisk together the half and half (or milk), eggs, grated onion, and Thousand Island dressing in a bowl until well combined.
7. Pour the mixture evenly over the filling in the pie plate.
8. Microwave on high for 9-11 minutes or until set in the center.
9. Let the quiche stand for 5 minutes before spreading the top with dressing.
Note: Do not force the heat; simply allow the dish to cook naturally to avoid any safety issues.
